top_signals.title: Overpricing Fed Rate Hikes Amid Potential Cuts
top_signals.summary: The segment discusses the current market sentiment that appears to be overestimating the likelihood of Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. Reference is made to the '12th blue dot,' a hypothetical addition to the Fed's dot plot that would represent a signal for rate cuts this year, attributed to former Fed official Warsh. This suggests that despite market expectations, JPMorgan analysts anticipate the Federal Reserve will keep rates on hold throughout the year. The discussion highlights the impact of these expectations on stock market performance, with stocks falling as investor focus shifts toward the possibility of policy changes signaled by Warsh. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Inflation peaked in May as energy prices fell in June, Kalshi traders think
top_signals.summary: Speculators on the prediction market platform give less than 30% odds to inflation peaking above 4.2% in 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
